findings.message: core-triad-migrations covers 5 edges: v0.1.0 to v0.2.0, v0.1.0 to v0.4.0, v0.2.0 to v0.4.0, v0.4.0 to v0.5.0, v0.5.0 to v0.6.0. Older triad-line lineage fully migratable.

v0.6.0-to-v0.7.0 migrator has sourceSchemaSet = CoreTriadSchemaSetIDs_v000_001_000.v000_006_000 and targetSchemaSet = CoreEntitySetSurface_v000_007_000.schemaSetSlug. This is the bridge across the triad-line to entity-line split.
core-entity-migrations covers 4 edges: v0.6.0 to v0.7.0 (bridge), v0.7.0 to v0.8.0, v0.8.0 to v0.9.0 (this session), v0.9.0 to v1.0.0 (this session). Entity-line fully migratable.

Directory listing returns only v0.7.0, v0.8.0, v0.9.0, v1.0.0. The core-entity-set line was born at v0.7.0; no v0.5.0 or v0.4.0 sets exist to migrate from. The bead's premise of 'extend backward' is structurally moot.
Chain is complete end-to-end from core-triad v0.1.0 -> v0.6.0 -> line-split -> core-entity v0.7.0 -> v1.0.0. No additional migrators are buildable because no earlier entity-line versions exist.
